behavior_summary: This script implements a simple hit handler for an item: when the object's U8P_LONGWEB::gotHit process runs it immediately destroys the item instance.
No dialogue, menus, flag changes, or branching logic are present — the behavior is pure cleanup in response to being hit.
Parameters are present on the U8P_LONGWEB::gotHit signature (uword, word) but are not used by this implementation.
notable_control_flow: U8P_LONGWEB::gotHit executes as a process and performs a single action: call Item::destroy(this).
Behavior is unconditional — there are no checks, branches, or flag manipulations before destruction.
The function is short and terminal for the item instance (cleanup/teardown semantics).
open_questions: What semantic meanings do the parameters (uword, word) carry in callers — e.g., attacker id, damage amount, or hit type? (inferred but not present here).
Is the unconditional destruction intended for all hit types, or is this a trimmed excerpt and additional logic exists elsewhere?
Are there other systems (inventory, quest flags) that expect notification before this item is destroyed?

main_function.name: U8P_LONGWEB::gotHit
main_function.purpose: Act as the item's hit handler and remove the item instance from the game when it receives a hit.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine as the U8P_LONGWEB::gotHit process for the item (process type 0x020C) — i.e., when the object is hit or damaged.

function_map.name: U8P_LONGWEB::gotHit
function_map.role: Hit handler / cleanup: destroys the current item instance when invoked.

function_map.name: Item::destroy
function_map.role: Engine/item API call to remove and free the item instance (called with this).

external_calls.purpose: Remove/free the item instance from the game world (cleanup of this object).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements a simple hit handler for an item: when the object's U8P_LONGWEB::gotHit process runs it immediately destroys the item instance.
